item.title: EasyJet bumps up ticket prices to offset rise in jet fuel costs
item.description: Airline reports half-year loss as customers show hesitance to book amid ongoing conflict in Iran

item.title: BT loses fewer broadband customers than feared
item.description: Telecoms group expands cost-cutting target as turnaround continues

item.title: SpaceX’s China-less IPO and other astronomical figures
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Jamie Dimon praises China for being ‘more consistent’
item.description: JPMorgan chief says US is ‘still a safe haven’ even though it ‘surprised people recently’

item.title: HMRC vs KFC: Buying the dip
item.description: Somehow, the British taxman is once again in an argument about chicken

item.title: The ECB’s window to ‘look through’ the energy shock has already shut
item.description: Read the May forecast from the FT’s Monetary Policy Radar team

item.title: Chris Hohn’s quest for eternal greatness
item.description: The UK’s most successful hedge fund manager has taken a spiritual turn

item.title: The Peckham shopping centre in the middle of Britain’s fight to build new homes
item.description: Developer Berkeley Group says rejected Aylesham plan is example of why housebuilders can “no longer invest” in the UK capital

item.title: Saudi Arabia stops new work for consultants as war rattles finances
item.description: Kingdom delays payments amid attempts to tighten controls on spending for Vision 2030 megaprojects

item.title: Big Tech software era is over, says top investor James Anderson
item.description: Former Baillie Gifford fund manager says spoils of AI war will flow to hardware suppliers

item.title: DR Congo races to halt spread of Ebola
item.description: Reporting breakdown and weak health systems in conflict-hit country allowed virus to go undetected for weeks

item.title: Europe’s best employers 2026: ranking
item.description: Employees across the continent were surveyed by the FT and Statista for this edition of the list

item.title: Starmer’s crisis lands on UK housebuilders’ doorstep
item.description: The sector is not in crisis but does face a cyclical slowdown, with a lot of hot air already coming out of stock prices

item.title: Protein boom drives ‘gravity-defying’ rally in whey prices
item.description: Byproduct of cheese industry has become a valuable commodity

item.title: The new art of war is just as bloody as the old
item.description: Armed drones and AI have transformed the frontline into treacherous kill zones comparable to the first world war

item.title: Barnacles and jellyfish infest ships trapped in the Gulf
item.description: Sea life could impede the ability of vessels to leave the region when the conflict ends

item.title: Is Chris Hohn Britain’s answer to Warren Buffett?
item.description: The billionaire hedge fund manager has deep convictions on finance, philanthropy — and, increasingly, faith

item.title: The benefits of an investment bubble
item.description: UK telecoms sector shows how splurge of money can destroy shareholder value but deliver consumer gains

item.title: Mitsotakis on Hormuz, Iran and how to escape a debt trap
item.description: Greek Prime Minister outlines his views on trade and security at a time of war

item.title: Samsung reaches last-minute deal to avert strike over AI riches
item.description: Work stoppage had threatened to disrupt Korean economy and global artificial intelligence boom

item.title: Asteroid mining and new billionaires: what’s in SpaceX’s IPO filing
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket-to-AI conglomerate lays out its planetary ambitions

item.title: Anthropic on track for first profitable quarter
item.description: AI lab set to hit milestone ahead of competitors OpenAI and xAI

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX sets out plans for biggest IPO in history
item.description: Satellite and AI company to list on Nasdaq under symbol SPCX

item.title: FirstFT: Nvidia reports better than expected revenue and forecasts in Q1 earnings
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: OpenAI’s IPO could come as early as this week and Xi warns of ‘the law of the jungle’ to Putin

item.title: SpaceX is all ’trucked up
item.description: But Elon Musk is not giving himself mates’ rates

item.title: Nvidia to return more than $80bn to shareholders as it reaps rewards of AI boom
item.description: World’s most valuable company reported better than expected revenue and forecasts

item.title: US state department watchdog investigates Gaza Humanitarian Foundation
item.description: Oversight body scrutinising how $30mn grant was spent by controversial non-profit that had US and Israeli backing

item.title: Oil drops nearly 6% as two China-bound supertankers cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Brent crude falls to about $105 a barrel as Asia-bound vessels raise hopes of resumption of energy flows

item.title: M&S boss slams ‘preposterous’ proposal to cap food prices
item.description: Stuart Machin’s comments come as government pushes supermarkets to sign up to voluntary price limits for key items

item.title: Zuckerberg promises no more ‘company-wide’ lay-offs after slashing jobs
item.description: Meta chief seeks to reassure disillusioned employees after culling 8,000 roles
